question: Statement: 'All students must wear ID cards on campus.' Conclusion: Students are expected to carry ID cards on campus.
solution: This is a direct restatement of the rule, so it follows.

question: Statement: 'The company launched a new phone yesterday.' Conclusion: The new phone will be a best-seller.
solution: Success is a prediction not supported by the statement.

question: Statement: 'Some employees attended the training.' Conclusion: All employees are interested in training.
solution: 'Some' does not justify 'all'.

question: Statement: 'Keep this medicine out of the reach of children.' Conclusion: The medicine may be harmful to children.
solution: The warning implies a risk to children — a necessary reason for the instruction.

question: Statement: 'Vegetable prices rose sharply this month.' Conclusion: Vegetables are costlier now than earlier this month.
solution: A direct restatement of the price rise.

question: Statement: 'The library will remain open till 10 pm during exams.' Conclusion: The library will be open at 9 pm during exams.
solution: Open till 10 pm means it is open at 9 pm.

question: Statement: 'Due to heavy rain, the match was postponed.' Conclusion: Rain can affect outdoor matches.
solution: The statement itself shows rain affecting a match, supporting the general point.

question: Statement: 'The new policy reduced office paper use by 30%.' Conclusion: The office used less paper after the policy.
solution: A 30% reduction means less paper — a restatement.

question: Statement: 'Only candidates above 18 can vote.' Conclusion: A 16-year-old cannot vote.
solution: 'Only above 18' excludes those under 18, so a 16-year-old cannot vote.

question: Statement: 'Our product is used by many households.' Conclusion: The product is the best in the market.
solution: 'Many use it' does not prove 'best' — an extreme, unsupported claim.

question: Statement: 'The government increased the health budget this year.' Conclusion: Health services will surely improve.
solution: More budget doesn't guarantee improvement; it needs extra assumptions.

question: Statement: 'Wear a helmet while riding a two-wheeler.' Conclusion: Helmets can improve rider safety.
solution: The advice implies helmets aid safety — its necessary purpose.

question: Statement: 'Rahul scored the highest marks in his class.' Conclusion: No one in his class scored more than Rahul.
solution: Highest means no classmate scored more — a restatement.

question: Statement: 'The train was delayed by two hours due to a signal fault.' Conclusion: All trains on that route are always late.
solution: One delayed train doesn't imply 'all always late' — extreme generalisation.

question: Statement: 'The school introduced online classes so that students in remote areas can study.' Conclusions: I. Some students live in remote areas. II. Online classes can reach remote students. Which follows?
solution: The stated purpose assumes remote students exist (I) and that online classes reach them (II); both are supported.

question: Statement: 'Prices of petrol have risen for the third straight month.' Conclusions: I. Petrol is costlier than three months ago. II. Petrol prices will fall next month. Which follows?
solution: I restates the sustained rise; II is an unsupported future prediction.

question: Statement: 'To reduce accidents, the city lowered the speed limit on highways.' Conclusion: High speed was linked to accidents in the city.
solution: Lowering speed to cut accidents implies speed was a factor — the reasoning behind the action.
trick: The remedy reveals the cause assumed.

question: Statement: 'Most of the participants completed the marathon.' Conclusions: I. Some participants did not complete it. II. Every participant completed it. Which follows?
solution: 'Most' means not all, so some did not complete (I); II contradicts 'most'.

question: Statement: 'The bakery sells out of bread by noon every day.' Conclusion: Demand for the bakery's bread is high.
solution: Consistently selling out by noon reasonably indicates high demand.

question: Statement: 'A notice says: Do not use the lift during a fire; use the stairs.' Conclusions: I. Lifts may be unsafe during a fire. II. Stairs are a safer option during a fire. Which follows?
solution: The instruction implies lifts are unsafe (I) and stairs are the safer alternative (II) during a fire.
trick: Safety notice implies both the risk and the alternative.
